summary: CSS-only iteration 12 validation: re-ran the canonical 5-suite regression bundle (test_client_chat_page + test_manager_chat_page + test_image_url_double_prefix_fix + test_cors_new_domain + test_cabinet_data_leak_fix). 80/80 PASS in 89s. Static-parsed App.js + CategoryChips.js with @babel/parser+jsx — both OK. CSS contract on /app/event-tool-source/src/styles/mobile.css inside @media (max-width: 768px) verified: `body .p-8 { padding: 0 !important }` present (line 95), `.fd-header ~ div[style*="background"][style*="f3f3f3"]` present (lines 97-98), `.category-chips-wrapper { margin-top: 0 !important }` present (lines 102-104). Total `padding: 0 !important` occurrences in file = 4 (>=4 inside media query as required).
action_items: User must re-run `yarn build && sudo cp -a build/. /var/www/event-tool-build/` on VPS to deploy the new CSS — in-pod source change cannot reach the deployed site by itself.
critical_code_review_comments: CSS fix relies on cascade specificity (`body .p-8`) and `!important` to override Tailwind's compiled `.p-8`. This is the correct pragmatic approach given the JIT-compiled selectors. Long-term, removing the `p-8` className from the EventPlannerPage outer wrapper in App.js (line 624) would be cleaner than fighting it with overrides.

Inline `background: #f3f3f3` in App.js around line 621 is being defeated by an attribute-selector override — also pragmatic but fragile. Consider moving this background into a CSS class so media queries can scope it cleanly without attribute-selector hacks.
test_credentials: vitokdrako@gmail.com / test123 via POST /api/event/auth/login (used inside the suites)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 12 is purely a CSS deploy issue. Regression bundle (80 tests) is the canonical smoke set; rerun it for any backend or CSS-only change. JS parse check via @babel/parser is fast (<1s) and worth keeping in the checklist for any App.js/CategoryChips.js edit. Remaining mobile-gap issue is on the user's VPS build pipeline, not in pod.
rca of the issue: User reported gray gap between sticky header and chips still visible on mobile after iteration 10. Iteration 11 only changed selector `.fd-header + div .p-8` which had ambiguous specificity vs Tailwind JIT compiled `.p-8`. Iteration 12 adds three more aggressive overrides inside @media (max-width: 768px): (1) `body .p-8 { padding: 0 !important }` defeats any cascade-order issue, (2) `.fd-header ~ div[style*="background"][style*="f3f3f3"]` targets the inline-styled gray container directly via attribute selector, (3) `.category-chips-wrapper { margin-top: 0 !important }` zeroes out any wrapper margin. Verification confirmed all three rules exist in source mobile.css. The remaining visibility of the gap on the user's site is purely a deployment/cache issue — the pod source CSS does not auto-propagate to /var/www/event-tool-build/. Mitigation: user runs `yarn build && sudo cp -a build/. /var/www/event-tool-build/`.
